text:
In the first space, I feel a quiet emptiness, almost as if I am paused amid anticipation. The sterile, open architecture, beanbags, and scattered furniture invite reflection or waiting, yet the tilted view unsettles me, making me question my footing and perspective. It is a liminal place—between action and inaction.

Moving to the next scene, a glow of vibrant color and digital text greets me; I am drawn into the realm of ideas and innovation. The display and monitors engage my curiosity, the written word echoing my own thoughts: seeing through obscured vision, the very sensation of uncertainty made manifest. I feel a sense of being prompted to question not only what I see, but how I see—reminded, in the midst of design and research, of my own searching mind.
